key_insight: DeepJEB++ builds a large, simulation-labeled 3D engineering dataset by augmenting in 2D latent space first, then transferring the validated results into 3D.
engineering_relevance: This enables scalable design-data generation for solid mechanics tasks where geometry, manufacturability, and physics labels must stay consistent.
method: Fine-tune a pretrained 2D latent diffusion model on multi-view jet-engine-bracket renders and generate new views via latent interpolation.
Use a vision-language-model filter to keep only manufacturable, geometry-consistent candidates.
Lift validated images to 3D meshes with a domain-adapted generative foundation model, then auto-detect load/bolt interfaces to assign FE labels.
evidence_signal: Starting from fewer than 400 seed designs, the pipeline produces 15,360 simulation-labeled 3D brackets, a 40x expansion, and evaluates quality via manufacturability, label fidelity against SimJEB, and distributional consistency.
watch_item: The pipeline depends on robust interface recognition and boundary-condition assignment after 3D reconstruction, which may be error-prone for more complex or less standardized geometries.
paper_title: DeepJEB++: Foundation Model-Driven Large-Scale 3D Engineering Dataset via 2D Latent Space Augmentation
